pdg_node.statement: (foot_sensing_state_in: object { lfoot_sensing_: bool, rfoot_sensing_: bool }, foot_sensing_command: object { lfoot_sensing: bool, rfoot_sensing: bool }) -> object { lfoot_sensing_: bool, rfoot_sensing_: bool }
pdg_node.informal_desc: Applies a pure state transition for left/right foot sensing flags, returning a new sensing state object.

declaration.name: foot_sensing_state_update
declaration.type_signature: (foot_sensing_state_in: object { lfoot_sensing_: bool, rfoot_sensing_: bool }, foot_sensing_command: object { lfoot_sensing: bool, rfoot_sensing: bool }) -> object { lfoot_sensing_: bool, rfoot_sensing_: bool }
declaration.docstring: Applies a pure state transition for left/right foot sensing flags, returning a new sensing state object.
declaration.conceptual_summary: Binary Channel State Overwrite Transition. Transforms an immutable binary state snapshot by applying a command vector that specifies target values for each boolean channel. The operation constructs a new state object with updated channel bits rather than mutating the input snapshot. This is a discrete finite-state transition with direct per-field assignment and no intermediate optimization or inference.. Applications: Control engineering: updating a two-actuator enable/disable latch in a sampled-time controller., Computational biology instrumentation: setting a two-channel acquisition mask for a measurement cycle., Distributed systems: applying a two-flag runtime feature-state update in immutable configuration snapshots., Digital communications: overwriting a two-bit mode register in protocol state progression.

pdg_node.statement: (mode_state_in: object { mode_: Mode, previous_mode_: Mode }) -> tuple[Mode, Mode]
pdg_node.informal_desc: Reads current and previous mode from immutable classifier state and exposes them as explicit outputs.

declaration.name: mode_snapshot_readout
declaration.type_signature: (mode_state_in: object { mode_: Mode, previous_mode_: Mode }) -> tuple[Mode, Mode]
declaration.docstring: Reads current and previous mode from immutable classifier state and exposes them as explicit outputs.
declaration.conceptual_summary: Lag-1 Discrete State Projection. Extracts two temporally adjacent categorical state tokens from an immutable composite snapshot and emits them as separate outputs. The mapping is a direct structural projection from a packed record to an explicit ordered pair representing present and immediately preceding state. No mutation, inference, or recomputation is performed; symbol identity is preserved exactly.. Applications: Finite-state controller diagnostics in robotics, exposing current and previous control regimes for transition validation, Communication protocol analyzers, surfacing current and prior parser states for handshake/sequence debugging, Compiler or interpreter pipeline tracing, reporting current and previous processing phases for execution observability, Industrial process automation monitoring, emitting current and prior machine operation states for rule-based safety checks
